top_findings.summary: The skill description indicates extensive access to sensitive browser data including browsing history, cookies, bookmarks, and network requests, which exceeds what is reasonably necessary for browser automation. While some access to tabs and page interaction is expected for automation, the ability to search/delete browsing history, audit/manipulate cookies, access bookmarks, and capture all network traffic provides excessive surveillance capabilities. Additionally, the inclusion of audio/TTS playback and desktop notification features extends beyond the core browser automation purpose, creating potential privacy risks through audio capture and unauthorized notifications.
